title: Tree Calculus
full_text:
Tree Calculus was discovered by Barry Jay. Check out his book and blog!

The demos on this website were developed by Johannes Bader.

See here for more background, resources and contact info.

 

 

Minimal
 

There is one operator в–і that computes whenever it is acting on three values.
Grammar: E ::= в–і | E E

 

Visually, в–і is a tree node and the application of E1 to E2 attaches
E2 to the root of E1 on the right. This forms trees like that in the picture above.
Values are natural binary trees.
We call their nodes leaves, stems or forks.

 

Practical consequences
 

рҹҡҖ Demo: Trivial, safe interpreters on any platform.

рҹҡҖ Demo: Good fit for cross-plat config generation.
 
 

 

Turing-complete
 

Combinatory logic with operators K and S is complete and we have:

 

1. K=в–і в–і

 

2. S x=в–і (в–і x)

 

Unlike О»-calculus, recursive functions can be represented as normal forms,
using fixpoint constructions like orange/brown in the picture above.
 
 

 

Reflective
 

triage{l,s,f}=в–і (в–і l s) f
performs case analysis on leafs, stems and forks.

 

We can represent natural number n as в–іnв–і.
The zero test is
triage{true,
Kfalse,
K2false}.

 

Since programs are also values, intensional programs can be self-applied, to achieve introspection and reflection.
The picture above shows program size, which computes its argumentвҖҷs number of nodes.
For instance, size size evaluates to 168.

 

Practical consequences
 

рҹҡҖ Demo: Abillity to serialize programs.

рҹҡҖ Demo: Simpler formulation of the halting problem.

рҹҡҖ Demo: Program analysis/optimization as a function.

рҹҡҖ Demo: Static vs dynamic typing? Just function calls.
 
 

 

Modular
 

Sub-terms are represented as sub-trees.
The size program pictured at the top of this page uses
triage
{
(+ 0),
О»u.
(+ size u),
О»uv.
(+ size u)
вҲҳ
(+ size v)
}
вҲҳ
(+ 1)
to count nodes recursively.

 

Practical consequences
 

рҹҡҖ Demo: Common functionality is easy to bootstrap.

рҹҡҖ Demo: Powerful programs need not be large trees.
 
 

 

Democratizing functions
